page: Amazon Web Services x8i.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling due to the dual-vCPU configuration. Single-threaded tasks perform well, as evidenced by strong scores in Geekbench single-core and single-threaded text processing, alongside strong gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, are weak. Memory bandwidth tests show a significant drop-off for uncached large-block operations, with read speeds at 20.29 GB/sec and write speeds at 16.61 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x8i.large offers a dense memory profile relative to its low compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. It is suitable for small-scale in-memory caching, lightweight database operations, single-threaded data processing, and text processing tasks. It is less suitable for highly parallelized compute jobs, large-scale compilation, or high-throughput web serving due to its constrained multi-core capacity and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 1 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C core running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory, resulting in a high 16 GB per vCPU ratio, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with high scores in single-threaded text processing and compression tasks, contrasted by weak multi-core and uncached memory bandwidth performance. With a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for small in-memory databases, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing applications that require substantial memory capacity without demanding high parallel CPU performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low compute requirements.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and strong single-threaded performance for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
bullet_points: 2 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 480 MB L3 cache
Strong single-core and single-threaded compression performance
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage
Optimized for small in-memory databases and caching layers
